segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Three days had passed since the dragon's death, and Thornwick still hadn't stopped smoking.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin had spent most of that time in fever dreams, his body fighting the scale's invasion while his mind spiraled through visions he couldn't hold. Even asleep, pain was his pulse -- the scale burned, a low ember ache that never cooled.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The bonding hadn't been clean; it rewrote him from the inside out. He'd burned so hot that Vex packed him in snow from shaded hollows just to stop him smoking.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: By the third morning, the fever broke.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale settled to its baseline: a thumb-length, hex-edged plate lying flush in his skin, translucent obsidian with ember-motes drifting inside, pulsing steady.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The filaments had spread -- not just toward wrist and elbow now, but creeping past the joint. Dark threads visible beneath skin that would never look quite normal again.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He flexed his fingers, testing. The embedded plate flexed with him. The filaments shifted like roots seeking deeper soil.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "You look less on fire today,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said from the doorway of the half-collapsed inn where they'd taken shelter. Her eyes didn't match -- one gold-hazel, one storm-grey -- the only inheritance from a mother who'd spent her life studying magic she wasn't supposed to touch.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Improvement or just denial?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: . She held a clay cup that steamed in the morning chill.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "A little."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The truth came easier after three days of the scale punishing every half-lie his fever-addled mind had tried.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Different now. Less like fire, more like... weight."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "The veins are spreading."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not a question. She'd been watching the progression, cataloging it with the same attention she gave potential threats and profitable opportunities.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He tugged his sleeve down. The cloth caught on the plate's raised edge -- a small, stupid reminder he would never hide it for long.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He'd tested it that morning out of stubbornness -- wrapped it, braced it, even tried steel.: linen wrappings had smoldered after an hour, and the leather bracer from the inn's abandoned supplies had conducted heat until it burned his skin, leaving blisters that still throbbed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Metal was worse -- the scale had flared white-hot the moment steel touched it, as if offended by the attempt at concealment.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Right,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, watching the sleeve ride back up.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Not ominous at all."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She handed him the cup -- broth made from dried vegetables and questionable meat, but hot and real.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Drink. You need to eat actual food today or you'll collapse halfway to wherever that magic compass in your arm wants to drag us."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He sipped. It tasted like survival -- not good, but good enough.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "You stayed."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "You didn't have to,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, surprised how small his voice sounded.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Investment protection."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She settled against the opposite wall, her own cup cradled in scarred hands.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Spent three days keeping you alive, forcing water down your throat when you tried to bite me, and listening to you speak fluent Draconic in your sleep -- which was deeply unsettling, by the way. Would be wasteful to let you die now."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale stayed warm. No sting. Close enough to truth -- she had stayed for practical reasons, even if other reasons were growing beneath the surface.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "The dreams,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "What did I say?"

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Nothing I could understand. But you said it with authority."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her mismatched eyes studied him.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "And that thing -- "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: a gesture at his arm

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: " -- lit up the whole room every time you started. Like sleeping next to a forge that randomly decided to perform a light show."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He'd noticed. The scale answered emotion as much as magic: hesitation brought the white-hot sting; fever dreams brought the full prismatic display. He was learning to school his thoughts, to keep them surface-level and practical, to not think too deeply about what he'd become.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale warmed slightly. It knew what he was doing.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "We should go back,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, pushing to his feet. His legs protested but held.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "To the crater. I need to see it with clear eyes."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Already scouted it twice."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: But Vex stood as well, checking her daggers from habit.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Nothing's changed. Dragon's still dead and crystallized. Path still leads to the mountains. Village still looks like someone melted it into modern art."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I need to see it anyway."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She shrugged.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Your funeral. Or mine, probably, since I'm following you."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They left the inn as morning light turned the transformed village into something almost beautiful. Glass-smooth walls caught sun and threw rainbows. Crystalline puddles reflected sky in fractured mirrors.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Even the destruction had an elegance to it -- the way catastrophe sometimes does, when you're far enough away from the dying.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: People watched them pass. Fewer than before -- half the survivors had already left for Millbrook, unable to stay in a place that wore its trauma like decoration. Those who remained were the stubborn or the broken, the ones with nowhere else to go or reasons to stay that outweighed sense.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A woman carrying a child saw Caelin's exposed forearm and made a warding sign.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: An old man simply stared, mouth working soundlessly.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "They're afraid,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex murmured.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I saved a child here."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "You did. Then you went to meet a dragon and came back changed."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She kept her voice low.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "People fear what they don't understand. That -- "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: a subtle gesture

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: " -- they definitely don't understand."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed warm. She was right, as usual.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They followed the path of scorched hedge and melted stone toward the crater. The destruction formed a clear line -- deliberate, purposeful -- leading away from the village center. Morning mist clung to the hollows, making the landscape seem unreal, halfway between memory and present.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Where the hedgerow ended, the crater waited.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She was gone. Not crystallised, not preserved -- gone entirely. The glass floor held only the ghost of her outline, a perfect dragon-shaped shadow scorched into the obsidian where ten thousand years of history had finally settled and gone dark.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The ember-motes in Caelin's scale drifted a little faster as he slid down the slope. He wondered if they knew.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "What are you looking for?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex called from the rim.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I don't know."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He crossed to the centre and crouched at the outline's edge. The glass was warm here -- just faintly, the last of it. His marked arm pulsed once in recognition, amber light catching nothing.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Thank you,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said quietly, not sure if it was gratitude or accusation. Maybe both.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The glass didn't answer.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Footsteps crunched above -- not Vex, different rhythm. Caelin turned, hand moving instinctively toward the sword he'd left at the inn.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A figure crested the crater's rim from the north side -- short, broad, trailing a small cloud of steam and the unmistakable tick-tick-whirr of clockwork in motion. Even at a distance, the mechanical clicking carried across the glass-smooth bowl with crystal clarity.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "That's either the world's loudest thief,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex called down, daggers already in hand,

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "or a very confused tinker."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The figure resolved as it descended: a dwarf, copper beard braided with gear-charms that glinted in the light, goggles pushed up on a wild-browed forehead revealing keen eyes beneath.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A pack that seemed more metal than leather rode his shoulders, bristling with tools and devices and things that hummed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He stopped twenty feet away, tilted his head at Caelin like a bird examining something shiny, and beamed with the particular joy of someone proving themselves right after a long argument.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Aha!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The dwarf's voice carried genuine delight.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Knew the readings couldn't be wrong. Well, they're usually wrong, but not about this. Never about this."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He unslung a brass contraption from his shoulder -- a brass contraption of spinning dials and trembling needles that twisted light in uneasy patterns. He pointed it at Caelin.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The device screamed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not metaphorically -- literally shrieked, a high metallic wail that sent birds scattering from the crater's rim and made Caelin's teeth ache.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The dwarf slapped it. The screaming became sparking. He slapped it again. Silence, except for an ominous ticking that suggested the device was either thinking or plotting revenge.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Brilliant,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he told the device with genuine affection. Then, to them:

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Thornik Bramblebrew. Artificer, engineer, and occasional brewer of exploding ale -- though that last bit's more accident than intention. You're the Ember-born, unless my instruments are lying. Which they don't, except when they do, but not about dragon-marks. Never about dragon-marks."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik's eyes went wide behind his pushed-up goggles.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "By my beard."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: His voice dropped to something like reverence.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "It recognized my kit."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He took a step closer, goggles sliding down over his eyes with a mechanical snick. The lenses whirred, adjusting, magnifying.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "May I? Grandfather wrote about this -- well, he wrote in blood, technically, which was dramatic even for him -- but if the resonance patterns match what he described, if the harmonic frequencies align, if the -- "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex shifted between them -- not blocking entirely, but making her presence felt.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Maybe ask before reaching for things embedded in people's arms. Novel concept, I know."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik held up both hands, device still humming.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Fair, fair. Wouldn't want to interfere with an active bonding anyway. Could cause sympathetic feedback loops."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He paused, considering.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Or explosions. Fifty-fifty odds, really, and explosions are only fun when they're intentional."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Comforting,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, instinctively covering the scale with his other hand. It pulsed warmer, as if objecting to being hidden even by his own flesh.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "What brings an artificer to a burned village?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex asked, still wary, still positioned to strike if needed.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Same thing that brought him, I'd wager."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik's enthusiasm dimmed slightly, replaced by something heavier -- old grief wearing new clothes.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "The dreams. My whole clan's been having them for months. Forges waking in the deep places where we sealed them. Constructs stirring in halls we'd forgotten. The old songs starting up again in chambers we'd filled with stone and regret."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He gestured at the dragon's crystalline corpse, at the transformed crater, at the morning light making art of catastrophe.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Then three nights ago -- the exact night she died -- every piece of sensing equipment in Deepstone Hold went mad at once. Every dial spinning. Every alarm screaming. Every blessed instrument pointing here, at this spot, saying 'something fundamental just changed.'"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik pulled a water-stained journal from his coat -- leather gone soft with age and handling, pages brittle at the edges, stained with substances that might have been blood or oil or tears.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "My grandfather died believing himself a fool for chasing legends. Spent his last gold on an expedition into the Emberpeaks, came back empty-handed and broken-spirited. Wrote in this -- in his own blood, mind you, because apparently sanity's optional in our family -- about hearing old songs, about seeing the pedestals of the Nine, about..."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He trailed off, shook his head, but his hands clutched the journal like it might escape.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "About warnings. Prices paid. Choices that haunt. He died thinking he'd failed. That he'd missed something vital. That he'd doomed us all by not understanding fast enough."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The dwarf's eyes met Caelin's, and the weight there aged him.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Point is, if he was right about any of it -- if even one page of his mad rambling was true -- then everything changes. The old alliances weren't myths. The Concord wasn't legend. And whatever's waking now..."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He glanced at the scale in Caelin's arm.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "It's already started."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale flared hot for just a moment -- not pain, but insistence. Share it. Tell him. The dragon had said to find others, to gather bearers. This dwarf had come seeking the same truth. His grandfather had died chasing it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin hated that the scale was probably right.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "The dragon spoke of nine relics,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, the words feeling too large for the smoking crater and morning light.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Forged in concordance. Nine bearers to claim them. And something beneath stone -- something called the Seal -- that has to stay sleeping."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik's face cycled through several emotions in rapid succession: shock, vindication, terror, and finally settling on manic determination.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "The Concord of Nine. Gods and ancestors and all the stone beneath, he was right."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He opened the journal with trembling hands, pages crackling.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Grandfather wrote about it. The great alliance -- dragons and dwarves forging together, nine artifacts to maintain the balance, one for each school of magic. But there were warnings too. About prices. About sacrifices. About -- "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: his voice dropped

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: " -- about what happens if the balance breaks."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "What happens?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex asked, climbing down into the crater now, curiosity overriding caution.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik looked up from the journal, and his expression was bleak.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "He didn't write it. Just left blank pages with one phrase: 'May none live to learn the answer.'"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Silence stretched between them, broken only by the soft ticking of Thornik's devices and the distant sound of Thornwick's survivors going about the grim business of salvage and departure.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed steady in Caelin's arm -- counting heartbeats, marking time, waiting for whatever came next.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A sound from the crater's rim made them all turn -- the clink of metal on stone, deliberate and measured. Not the mechanical clicking of Thornik's devices but something heavier. Purposeful.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A horse stood at the crater's edge, steam rising from its flanks. Its rider dismounted with practiced ease, though Caelin noted the stiffness in her movements -- old injuries, poorly healed, the kind that never quite stopped aching.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She wore armor that had once been white and gold but now looked sun-bleached and road-worn, the kind of patina that came from months in the saddle without proper maintenance.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not from neglect, he realized, but from lack of time -- or perhaps lack of caring about appearances when survival took precedence.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I'm looking for Elder Gareth,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she called down, her voice carrying the formal cadence of someone used to announcing themselves in halls of power. Then her gaze landed on Caelin's exposed forearm -- the sleeve had ridden up again during his conversation with Thornik -- and she stopped mid-sentence.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale reacted immediately.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not the white-hot sting of lies or the prismatic shimmer of wild magic. This was different -- a steady, golden-ember glow that Caelin had never seen before. The light pulsed from the embedded plate like a heartbeat made visible, warm and... welcoming? No, not quite. Recognizing.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The woman's hand moved to her sword hilt -- not in threat but in reflex -- and her palm began to glow. Soft silver-gold light flickered across her skin like a candle struggling in wind, uncertain but persistent.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "What are you?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she asked, and the question held no accusation, only genuine need to understand.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Cursed,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, because it felt more honest than

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "chosen."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Dragon-marked,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik added helpfully, his device already whirring as it tracked the interaction.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "First verified case in three generations. Possibly longer. My readings indicate a stable harmonic resonance between -- "

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "I wasn't asking you."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: But her grip on the sword relaxed fractionally. The light in her palm didn't fade, but it steadied somewhat, silver and gold still dancing their uneasy dance but no longer quite so frantic.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She descended into the crater with care, testing each step on the glass slope before committing her weight. Up close, Caelin could see the exhaustion in her face -- the kind that went deeper than lack of sleep, that spoke of questions without answers and certainty turned to sand.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Serana Valeblade,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, the formality a shield or perhaps just habit.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Paladin of the Silver Dawn. I came to offer aid to the survivors, but..."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She looked around at the glass-fused crater, the crystalline dragon, the morning light making art of devastation.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "This is beyond healing. This is divine reckoning."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Just dragon fire,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said from where she leaned against the crater wall, affecting casual despite the tension in her shoulders.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Very enthusiastic dragon fire, admittedly, but nothing divine about it."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "You're wrong."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana's voice carried absolute certainty -- the kind that came from hearing gods speak. Then that certainty cracked, and something raw showed through.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "At least... I think you're wrong. I used to be sure. Now..."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She closed her eyes briefly, and when she opened them, the exhaustion was even clearer.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Since the dragon's death my god speaks with two voices -- one preaching mercy, one sacrifice. I used to know which was truth. Now I don't."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She looked at Caelin, at the scale pulsing its steady golden rhythm in time with her flickering light.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "So I came seeking answers. Or at least clarity. Someone who could tell me if my god has fractured or if I have."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her jaw set.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Instead I find... that."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed again -- ember and silver-gold, fire and holy radiance, beating in an alternating pattern like a shared heartbeat. One pulse of gold from the scale, one flicker of silver from her palm, back and forth in perfect synchronization.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "The gods know,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik breathed, watching the synchronized pulsing with wide eyes. His device had stopped its usual clicking and settled into a low, resonant hum.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "They know what's waking. That's why her guidance splits -- divine will fracturing under the weight of impossible choices."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "What choices?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana demanded, her hand dropping from her sword to press against her chest where the Silver Dawn's symbol hung.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "What could possibly justify telling a paladin to both protect and sacrifice? To both shepherd and... and cull?"

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "The Concord,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said quietly, and suddenly all the manic energy had drained from him, leaving only the weight of inherited knowledge.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Grandfather wrote about it in the margins. The old alliance required... balance. Sometimes protection meant destruction. Sometimes healing meant cutting away the infected flesh before it could spread."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He looked at Serana with something like sympathy.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Your god isn't fracturing, lady paladin. Your god is preparing you for choices where both commands will be true at once. Where protecting some means sacrificing others. Where keeping the greater balance requires breaking lesser ones."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "That's not -- "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana started, then stopped. The silver-gold light in her palm flared brighter for a moment, as if in confirmation, and she looked at it with something between betrayal and understanding.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "That's not the Silver Dawn's teaching. We don't trade lives like currency. We don't choose who deserves protection and who doesn't."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Maybe you didn't have to before,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, surprisingly gentle.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Maybe the choices were always clear enough that mercy and pragmatism pointed the same direction. But if something's changing -- if something sealed is waking -- maybe the easy choices are over."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed warmer in Caelin's arm, but didn't sting. Truth, whether welcome or not.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana was quiet for a long moment, staring at her glowing palm, at the scale answering it with golden light, at the dragon's crystalline corpse bearing silent witness to transformation and consequence.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "What did she tell you?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana asked finally, looking at Caelin.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "The dragon. Before she died. What did she say was coming that could fracture divine guidance itself?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin flexed his marked arm, feeling the filaments shift beneath skin, the embedded plate counting heartbeats with patient precision.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "She said nine relics were forged to maintain balance. That nine bearers are needed to claim them. And that something called the Tenth -- something sealed beneath stone -- has to stay sleeping."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He met her eyes.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "She said if the Nine fail, if the balance breaks, then what's buried will wake. And it's wrong for the breathing world."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "The Tenth,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana repeated, and her silver-gold light guttered like a candle in wind.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "My prayers this morning... when I asked what I should do, what path I should follow... the answer came in two voices."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She swallowed hard.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "One said find the Nine, keep the chain. The other said some chains deserve breaking."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Which voice was louder?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik asked.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Neither. Both. They spoke over each other, equally strong, equally certain."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She looked lost.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "How do I serve my god when my god speaks with two mouths?"

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "You choose,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said bluntly.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "You pick which voice sounds more like the deity you swore to serve, and you follow that one. The other voice -- "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she shrugged

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: " -- might not be your god at all. Might be something else trying to sound like it."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale flared white-hot against Caelin's skin -- sudden, searing. He gasped, cradling his arm.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "What?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex spun toward him.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "What did I say?"

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Not you."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He gritted his teeth against the pain.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "The scale. It's warning -- "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The obsidian plate shifted, and for the first time since bonding, black-violet bled through the translucent surface like ink in water. The ember-motes inside swirled faster, agitated, and the pulse went irregular -- fast, then slow, then hard.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Corruption warning,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said, his device suddenly screaming again. He slapped it silent and checked the dials with growing alarm.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Three signatures. No, four. Strong ones. Closing fast from different vectors."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "What are they?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana's hand went back to her sword, and this time she drew it. Silver-gold light cascaded down the blade like liquid sunrise.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "If I'm reading this right?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik's voice had lost all its cheer.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Whatever was drawn here after the dragon died. Whatever's been hunting these mountains for something it can finally smell."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale's black-violet bloom intensified. The irregular pulse hammered against Caelin's nerves -- wrong wrong wrong in a rhythm that made his teeth ache.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "And these readings..."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik tapped his device as if hoping it was malfunctioning.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "These don't match anything living."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A scream rose from the direction of the village -- sharp, terrified, and cut short with sickening abruptness.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They ran.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Boots on glass, then dirt, then cobblestone still warm from dragon-fire three days old. The transformed village seemed to resist their passage, crystalline surfaces slick and treacherous, fused walls creating dead ends where paths used to run.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale's black-violet warning pulsed harder with each step, and Caelin felt something he hadn't felt since the bonding: fear not his own. The scale was afraid. Or warning him to be. Or both.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They burst into the village square and stopped.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A figure stood over the fallen -- someone Caelin recognized, vaguely, from three days ago. A trader, maybe, or a traveler who'd come seeking news of the dragon and found catastrophe instead. The body was intact, clothes recognizable, face still human.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: But the eyes were wrong.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Black from lid to lid. No white, no iris -- just obsidian darkness that reflected no light and seemed to drink the morning sun.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: And the shadow.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gods, the shadow.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It moved independent of the figure, stretching and bending in directions that had nothing to do with where the light fell. Tendrils of darkness writhed around the figure's feet, tasting the air like serpents, reaching toward the living with obvious hunger.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik's device had stopped screaming and started weeping -- a low, mournful tone that resonated in Caelin's chest, in his bones, in the scale's frantic black-violet pulse.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The corrupted figure turned toward them with liquid grace, head tilting at an angle just wrong enough to trigger every instinct that said predator.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: When it spoke, three voices emerged braided together -- the person it had been, something ancient and cold, and underneath both, the sound like stone grinding stone.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Ember-born,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: it said, and smiled with too many teeth.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "The Seal remembers. The Seal hungers."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Two more emerged from between buildings -- moving with that same wrong fluidity, shadows writhing like living things, black eyes drinking light.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Behind the wagons!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elder Gareth's voice bellowed from across the square.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Get the children behind the wagons!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana's sword flared brighter, silver-gold light casting hard shadows.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Positions!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale's black-violet bloom filled Caelin's entire forearm, pulsing so hard it hurt. And beneath the warning, beneath the fear, something else:

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Purpose.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The Dragon's Ember stirred in his chest -- not called, not yet, but ready. Waiting for the moment when warning became action, when fear became fuel.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The shadow-thralls took a step forward, moving in perfect synchronization.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Three of them,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, daggers appearing in her hands like magic tricks.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Four of us. I've had worse odds."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Five,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana corrected, stepping forward with her blade held steady despite the fear Caelin could see in her eyes.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Stay behind me."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik set his pack down with surprising gentleness, and when he stood again, he held a fat brass cylinder in one hand and had reached back to unhook the twin axes from his shoulders.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Right then,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, and his voice held something almost like joy beneath the terror.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Let's see if Grandfather's warnings were complete. Avert your eyes, lovely people."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The shadow-thralls smiled their too-wide smiles and charged.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The fight was short and ugly.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana hit first -- training overriding fear, muscle memory carrying her forward when thought might have frozen her. Her blade traced an arc of silver-gold light that cut through the air like a falling star, and where it met shadow-tether, the darkness screamed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not metaphorically. The shadows shrieked with voices that shouldn't exist, writhing away from the divine radiance like living things recoiling from fire. The thrall she'd struck staggered, its movements suddenly uncoordinated, as if strings had been cut.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "The light severs them!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana called, her voice steady despite the horror.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Aim for the shadows, not the bodies!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The corrupted figure knitted itself back together wrong -- limbs jerking, spine bending in directions spines shouldn't bend -- but it was slower now, damaged, the black in its eyes boiling like tar on summer stone.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik didn't wait for a second demonstration. He snapped a rune on the brass cylinder's base and hurled it into the gap between two thralls. It clattered across cobblestone with a sound like manic laughter -- tick-tick-tick -- and detonated.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The explosion wasn't fire. It was force -- a concussive whump that picked up loose debris and flung it outward in a perfect sphere. Shockwave, grit, and a flash bright enough to paint shadows on walls. The thralls flinched back, shadows fragmenting, bodies stumbling.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Ha!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik bellowed, already reaching for his axes.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "That's the good batch! Patent pending! Please don't steal my design!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex didn't waste breath on commentary. She slid in low while the thralls were still reeling from Thornik's blast, moving with the fluid economy of someone who'd learned to fight in alleys where hesitation meant death. Two quick strikes -- precise, surgical, merciless.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Hamstring on the first. The thrall's leg buckled, dropping it to one knee.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Kidney on the second as it turned toward her. The blade found the gap between ribs like it had eyes of its own.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Less talking,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, already spinning away from the third thrall's grasping reach.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "More dying. Preferably them, not us."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The third thrall lunged past Serana's guard, moving faster than something that broken should manage, straight for Caelin.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale ignited.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not with warning this time. With demand. The black-violet corruption bloom inverted instantly -- from warning to weapon, fear to fuel. Heat surged up Caelin's arm, into his chest, and the Dragon's Ember answered.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He raised his right hand -- instinct, not thought -- and called fire.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Dragonfire came.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not the careful flames he'd shaped to save Mira, but something older. Rawer. A focused cone of heat that turned the air glass-hot and sent the thrall reeling backward, skin blistering, shadow shrieking as it burned.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The thrall's movements became frantic, uncoordinated. Whatever was puppeting the body didn't understand fire -- or perhaps remembered it too well, knew it from before stone and sealing and the long dark beneath mountains.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Then the cost hit.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale flared white-hot -- pain branding every nerve -- then cycled through prismatic light to an angry red that hammered with his pulse. Vision tunneled, breath coming ragged.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He'd pushed too hard, used too much, let the Ember have too free a rein.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Pain tore up his arm in waves, each pulse worse than the last. His knees started to buckle.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana stepped through him -- literally shoulder-checked him backward out of the line of engagement -- and brought her blade down in a clean arc that finished the staggered thrall.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Silver-gold light washed through the thing's center, and it fell in pieces that began dissolving into smoke before they hit ground.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik met the last thrall with a roar and axes -- no gadgets now, just dwarf-forged steel and ancestral fury. The first axe bit through a corrupted shoulder. The second took it in the side.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Back to the pit with ye!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The thrall collapsed, shadows fragmenting like shattered glass.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They stood in the scorched square, panting, as silence fell heavy and absolute. Around them: smoke, crying from somewhere distant, the groan of a damaged wagon axle, the smell of charred flesh and something else -- something chemical and wrong, like copper mixed with rot.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin sank to his knees, cradling his marked arm. The scale pulsed its angry red throb, counting the cost of power used too desperately. Each pulse sent fresh agony through the filaments.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He'd known there would be pain -- the dragon had warned him -- but knowing and experiencing were different countries entirely.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Breathe,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said, kneeling beside him but not touching -- as if she understood that contact would only make it worse.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "The backlash will fade. Just breathe through it."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "How do you know?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he managed through gritted teeth.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "I don't. But it's what I'd want someone to tell me."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her honesty was almost comforting.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex was already checking the fallen bodies -- quick, professional, making sure the dead stayed dead.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "These were people,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, and something in her voice was harder than usual.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Probably from the refugee caravans. Maybe from Thornwick itself."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Not people anymore,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said grimly, wiping his axes on a piece of torn cloth. He bent to examine his device, which had finally stopped its mournful weeping.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Shadow corruption. Seal-touched, if Grandfather's notes are accurate. The thing beneath uses them like... like tools. Puppets with meat."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He looked up, and his expression was bleak.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "And these weren't scouts. My readings show they were lures."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Lures for what?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana asked, though her tone suggested she already knew the answer wouldn't be good.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "For whatever's coming next. To see who answers. To see who can fight."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik tapped a dial that still spun lazily.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "And we just showed it exactly what we're capable of."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Meaning more will come,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex finished. She stood, daggers still in hand, scanning the ruined square like she expected attack from every shadow.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "They always do. News travels fast among monsters."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elder Gareth emerged from behind a wagon, two children clutched in the crooks of his arms, his face grey with exhaustion and fear. Other survivors followed -- perhaps twenty in total, those who hadn't fled yet or couldn't, clinging to each other and what remained of their home.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "We cannot hold this place,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth said, and the words seemed to cost him.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "If they followed the dragon's fire, if they're drawn by what you carry -- "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: his eyes found Caelin's still-glowing scale

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: " -- they'll follow you. And we cannot survive another attack."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The flame-path flared at the edge of Caelin's vision, suddenly visible despite the pain, tugging like a hooked nerve toward the Emberpeaks. Had it always pulled this insistently, or had claiming his attention in front of threats made it more demanding?

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "You want us to leave,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said. Not a question.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: "I want you to lead them away."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth's voice was firm despite the tremor in his hands.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Draw whatever's hunting you toward the mountains, away from the caravan route to Millbrook. Give us time to evacuate what's left."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "That's -- "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana started, then stopped. Her silver-gold light flickered uncertainly.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "That's asking them to walk into danger to protect strangers."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "I'm asking them to do what they were going to do anyway."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth looked at each of them in turn.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "The mark pulls him. The path leads to the mountains. His purpose isn't here -- it's forward, toward whatever the dragon bound him to find."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: His gaze settled on Caelin.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Am I wrong?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed warm agreement. No sting. The old man was right -- Caelin would follow the flame-path regardless. The only question was whether he'd lead danger toward innocents or away from them.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "No,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin admitted, pushing to his feet despite the lingering pain.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "You're not wrong."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Then go. Today. Now, before more come."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth shifted the children in his arms, their faces buried against his shoulders.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "We'll head south to Millbrook. You head east to the Emberpeaks. Separate paths mean separate targets."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "He's not wrong,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said quietly.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Strategically sound. Cold, but sound."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik was already shouldering his pack, checking straps and buckles with practiced efficiency.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Been planning to head that direction anyway. My instruments have been screaming about something in those mountains since this all started. Might as well investigate with company instead of dying alone and unmourned."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "I came seeking answers,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said slowly, her hand on her sword hilt, her divine light still flickering between commands.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Perhaps the answers aren't here. Perhaps they're where you're going."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She looked at Caelin.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "If my god's guidance splits over the Nine, then maybe I need to see the Nine myself to understand which voice to follow."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "So we're doing this,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Following the glowing destiny road into certain danger based on a dead dragon's cryptic instructions. Grand."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed steady now -- the angry red had faded to something closer to its baseline coal-glow, though the filaments still ached.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The black-violet corruption warning had dissipated entirely with the thralls' destruction, but Caelin knew it would return. The Seal knew they were coming now. It had tasted their power and found them wanting to test again.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "We leave within the hour,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, and even he heard how his voice had changed -- less question, more command. The scale approved with a pulse of warmth.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Gather what supplies you can. Travel light but prepare for weather and combat both."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Listen to him giving orders,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex murmured, but there was something almost fond beneath the sarcasm.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Three days with a magic tattoo and he thinks he's a commander."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "The mark commands,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said, surprisingly serious.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "He's just learning to speak its language."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They dispersed to prepare -- Thornik to check his devices, Serana to tend the wounded one last time before departing, Vex to

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "acquire"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: whatever supplies the village could spare and wouldn't miss.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin stood in the square as survivors began loading wagons, the work of evacuation resuming around him like he was a stone in a stream. He looked at his marked arm, at the scale that had settled back to its steady pulse, at the filaments mapping his skin in dark tracery.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Mira appeared at his elbow -- silent as a ghost, still clutching his ruined cloak from three days ago. She looked up at him with eyes too old for her age.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "You're leaving,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "I have to."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Because of what the dragon gave you."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not a question. She reached out and touched the scale with one small fingertip, and it pulsed gentle warmth -- the first time since the fight it hadn't caused pain.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Will you keep the rhyme safe?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin asked.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "The one the dragon sang?"

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Every word,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she promised.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "And when you come back, I'll say it for you again so you remember why you left."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "If I come back."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale stung. White-hot, sharp, brief.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Mira's eyes widened, but she didn't pull her finger away.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "It wants you to tell the truth,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said with a child's directness.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "It does."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Then tell the truth."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He knelt so they were eye-level.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I don't know if I'll come back. The path is long and leads to dangerous places. But if I do what I'm meant to do -- if I find what I'm meant to find -- then places like Thornwick will be safer. Children won't lose their homes. Dragons won't come searching."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale stayed warm. Truth accepted.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Mira hugged him with fierce strength, then pulled back and pressed something into his hand -- a river stone, worn smooth, with a hole through its center.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "For luck. Papa said stones with holes are lucky because they've been blessed by water long enough to let light through."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Thank you,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, closing his fingers around it. The stone was warm from her pocket, smooth and real and ordinary in the best way.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She ran back to the wagons before he could say more, and he watched her go -- another person he was leaving behind, another reason to succeed written in the set of a child's shoulders.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: An hour later, they assembled at the eastern edge of the village where the flame-path pulled strongest. Five of them now -- Caelin, Vex, Thornik, Serana, and their packs of hastily gathered supplies. Behind them, Thornwick smoked and evacuated.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Ahead, the Emberpeaks rose blue and distant and patient.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The flame-path stretched clear and bright in Caelin's sight, unmistakable as a road paved with light only he could see.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "So we just walk toward the mountains,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Following something invisible."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "That's the plan,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin confirmed.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Terrible plan."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: But she adjusted her pack and checked her daggers one more time.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I've followed worse."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana touched the symbol at her throat, lips moving in silent prayer. When she opened her eyes, the silver-gold light flickered but held steady.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Together then. For whatever answers wait ahead."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "For answers,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik agreed.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "And for Grandfather's vindication, may the old fool rest easier knowing he wasn't mad after all."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They set out together, leaving Thornwick's smoke behind. The flame-path led them across fields where ash still drifted, toward foothills that climbed into mysteries older than kingdoms.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed its steady rhythm against Caelin's skin -- counting breaths, marking steps, pulling him inexorably forward.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Behind them, Elder Gareth watched from the last wagon, one hand raised in blessing or farewell.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Ahead, the mountains waited with patient, geological indifference.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Five people,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, not quite to anyone.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Following a road only one of us can see."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Seven,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik corrected, patting his pack.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Grandfather's notes count."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nobody argued. The flame-path led on. They followed.
